meta.title: Vendula Krajíčková | Front-end developer - React, TypeScript, JavaScript and Next.js, Prague
meta.description: I'm Vendy :), a frontend developer building websites and web apps in React using TypeScript, JavaScript and Next.js.
meta.ogTitle: Front-end developer - React, TypeScript, JavaScript and Next.js, Prague
meta.ogDescription: I develop websites and web applications in React using TypeScript. I code Figma designs into HTML/CSS with JavaScript elements.

hero.title: Hi 👋, I'm
hero.name: Vendy :)
hero.subtitle: Frontend developer
hero.descriptionRowFirst: I develop websites and applications in React using TypeScript.

p1.pre: Frontend developer of websites and web applications in

text.p2: I code Figma designs into responsive HTML/CSS and add interactive features.
text.p3: My next goal? Next.js – a natural extension of what I already know well from React.

p1.post: , that visitors directly see and use.

text2.p2: Designing and creating React components with emphasis on reusability across the project.
text2.p3: Coding and converting Figma designs into dynamic content that comes alive in the browser.

1.desc: I meet deadlines and often deliver results earlier than required.

2.desc: I don't overlook details – I tolerate only 1px!

3.desc: I feel more confident with a teammate, but I can also work independently.

4.desc: Feedback? I like to listen, it's a motivation to improve my skills.

contact.title: Currently not hunting, but the brave can knock.
contact.text: ... involvement in a short-term project or a new connection in the field? Bring it on!

educations.title: Courses and Certificates
educations.description: ... I continue to educate myself and expand my skills.

footer.paragraphStart: I don't rest on my laurels and also engage in smaller projects. Check out my
footer.paragraphEnd: , where I have these projects pinned.

skills.title: Skills and Tools
skills.description: ... that I use in my projects.

1.name: Expo burza Pardubice
1.description: Web with information about a one-day event, coded using HTML and Sass. I used the Eleventy templating system to generate static pages. The project is the final work of the Become a Coder course.

2.name: Winery
2.description: Web with a wine catalog, where users can filter products and submit a reservation form. I programmed the web in JavaScript and used Webpack for its build. The catalog data is stored in a separate file as an array of objects. I worked on this project as part of the JavaScript 2 course.

3.name: Movie Database
3.description: Web with a movie database, allowing users to search and view movie details. I programmed the application in React and fetch data from a publicly available API. I developed this project as part of the ReactGirls Advent Coding Challenge in collaboration with Lenovo.

4.name: Words Game
4.description: Web for practicing English vocabulary with a choice of 3 modules and the ability to change their default settings. I programmed the application in React and used Supabase for data management. This project was realized as part of the ReactGirls Mentoring Program.

5.name: Personal Website and Portfolio
5.description: Website showcasing my projects and skills. I initially converted the static HTML/Tailwind CSS version into React with dynamic content. In the next phase, I added TypeScript to the existing project.

6.name: Superquiz
6.description: Quiz application with multiple-choice questions. I programmed the static HTML/CSS design in React with TypeScript and used Supabase for data management. I extended the original desktop version to be responsive for smaller devices.

7.name: XXXMuck
7.description: Simple e-shop with a product catalog. I fetch data from an API. Each product has its own detailed page with an interaction button. I styled the application using Tailwind CSS and daisyUI. For dark/light mode switching, I used the NextUI library.

1.name: How to Web
1.description: In a fictional project I refreshed the basics of HTML and CSS. I learned to use Developer tools, render SVG icons via sprite technique, build responsive layouts across devices using flexbox and organize CSS with Sass by splitting code into smaller parts.

2.name: Become a Coder
2.description: I learned to create responsive pages, use advanced layout techniques – flexbox and CSS grid, organize CSS with the Sass preprocessor and split code into smaller parts, and create site content using the Eleventy static site generator with templates.

3.name: JavaScript 1
3.description: Basics of programming, I can manipulate elements in the DOM, use event principles and respond to user input, and work with media on the page. I am capable of creating a simple application and subsequently publishing it through my GitHub account.

4.name: JavaScript 2
4.description: I mastered the basics of creating frontend applications in pure JavaScript. I have practical knowledge of communicating with REST APIs. I can use basic building blocks such as components, authentication, and client-side routing. I am capable of creating projects based on Webpack.

5.name: ReactGirls Mentoring Program
5.description: Programming my own project, where I advanced my skills in developing applications in React. During the project, I had an assigned mentor who provided valuable advice and regularly conducted code reviews.

6.name: React 1 - Basics
6.description: I refreshed the basics of JavaScript and ES6+ syntax. I understand the fundamental principles of React. I can create a simple application composed of components that fetch data from an API, and work with useState, useEffect, and React Router.

7.name: Basics of TypeScript
7.description: I mastered the basics of TypeScript and its type system, simple data modeling, project setup in TypeScript including bundling, basics of parametric types (generics), and recommended practices for developing web applications in TypeScript and React.

8.name: React 2 - Tools and Techniques
8.description: I mastered using TypeScript in React applications. I gained a deeper understanding of React and the component lifecycle. I learned to create custom hooks and advanced state management methods using useContext. I can handle forms and validate data using Formik. I became familiar with writing unit tests and testing through Vitest.

9.name: Basics of Next.js
9.description: Creating multi-page applications using the Pages Router and dynamic segments in the URL. Styling methods: CSS/Sass, Styled Components, Tailwind CSS, CSS-in-JS. Data rendering and fetching: getServerSideProps (SSR), getStaticPaths (SSG), Automatic Static Optimization. Image optimization. Awareness of full-stack application development through API Routes and authentication setup.

10.name: Figma
10.description: I familiarized myself with the Figma environment and its basic features. I understand text styling, adding colors and shadow effects to elements. I work with auto layout for efficient element arrangement. I create components and their variants, design wireframes and functional prototypes according to specifications. For clear style management across the design, I use Figma Variables.
